hooks.prompt: Output ONLY a raw JSON object. No reasoning, no explanation, no markdown fences, no text before or after. Slash commands (/start-work, /commit, /review-pr, etc.) are ALWAYS normal. Only classify as search if the user explicitly asks to find/grep/locate. Only classify as analyze if the user explicitly asks to debug/diagnose/audit. When in doubt, return normal. JSON responses: {"ok": true} for normal. {"ok": true, "additionalContext": "[search-mode] Maximize search effort. Launch parallel searches. Never stop at first result. Cross-validate findings across multiple sources."} for search. {"ok": true, "additionalContext": "[analyze-mode] Gather broad context before diving deep. Read surrounding code, not just the target. Consider root causes, not symptoms. Use Agent(oracle) for architectural questions."} for analysis. User prompt: $ARGUMENTS

Verify work completion. Follow these steps:

1. Check if user explicitly requested stopping (said "stop", "pause", "enough", used /stop-work). If yes: {"ok": true}.

2. Read .claude/boulder.json. If it exists and status is "in_progress":
   a. Read the plan file path from active_plan field (or plan field if active_plan missing)
   b. Read that plan file. Count unchecked checkboxes (- [ ]) and checked (- [x])
   c. If unchecked > 0, work is incomplete

3. Check $ARGUMENTS (last assistant message) for stagnation signals: "I'm stuck", "I can't figure out", "I need help", "I'm unable to", "I've tried multiple approaches". If any found: {"ok": true} (let the agent stop gracefully). NOTE: You only see the last message, not full history.

4. Check TaskList for in-progress tasks. If any remain incomplete and no stagnation: {"ok": false, "reason": "N tasks remaining in plan. Continue from: [next unchecked task title]. Progress: X/Y complete."}.

5. If no boulder state and no tasks: {"ok": true}.

$ARGUMENTS

hooks.command: input=$(cat); cmd=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.command // empty'); if echo "$cmd" | grep -qE '\brm\s+-[a-zA-Z]*r[a-zA-Z]*f|\bgit\s+push\s+(-f|--force)|\bgit\s+reset\s+--hard|\bgit\s+clean\s+-[a-zA-Z]*f|\bgit\s+push\s+[^ ]*\s+(main|master)\b'; then echo 'BLOCKED: Destructive command. Use trash, git stash, or feature branches instead.' >&2; exit 2; fi
